purpose: Define source-safe preview validation for changelog entries and notifications created when tenant/project rules change, without sending real notifications, publishing real changelog, calling Make/Gemini or writing production.
rules: A rule change can create a preview changelog entry, but it must not publish or send notifications without human approval.
Audience must be role/segment based; do not store raw recipient phone or email.
High-impact changes require reviewedByRef and approvedByRef before any future notification.
Breaking changes must link to migration/rollback context from rule versioning.
Academia-impacting changes must create academyUpdateRef or academy_update_required status.
Notification drafts are preview only and must route through notification outbox/email mailbox future gates.
Do not mark sent, delivered, synced or published while gates are off.
Do not include secrets, tokens, private links, raw contact data, bank data, DPI/document data or raw message bodies.
claudePrototypeImpact: Product updates/changelog UI must show draft/review/approved preview, not published/sent unless gates are active.
Do not show email/WhatsApp notification sent for rule changes while providers are pending.
Show audience by role/segment, not raw recipients.
Rule changes that affect operations should surface pending Academia/manual updates.
High-impact rule changes need visible human review and approval states.

newLessons: How a rule change becomes a changelog entry
Who must be notified by role
Why notification drafts are not sends
How to approve or block a high-impact change
interactiveChecklists: Before drafting notifications for a rule change

hardStops: Do not publish real changelog from preview.
Do not send email, WhatsApp or Make notifications from preview.
Do not store raw recipients, raw bodies or private links.
Do not mark roles informed without future confirmation gate.
Do not close Academia update required without human review.
Do not write Firestore or production state from this preview.
